text:
In a small village nestled among rolling hills, lived a grumpy old man named Bartholomew. Bartholomew had lived there all his life save for one adventurous day. One day, he heard whispers of an enchanted forest that pulsed with magical energy.

Curiosity gnawed at him. He gathered all his courage, donned an old cloak that smelled of mold, and set off. The forest was larger than anything he’d ever seen, with sunlit glades that twinkled like stars. Birds sang melodies none had ever heard, and flowers bloomed in colors he couldn’t name.

Guided only by the stars and his keen sense of direction, Bartholomew ventured deeper. He stumbled upon an old, weathered tree with branches like arms greeting him. It whispered secrets as he sat beneath it, his heart light despite the loneliness.

After a week, feeling strangely rejuvenated, Bartholomew decided to head back. But he paused at a clearing where
